content:
# Leviticus Chapter 16 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 67v–68r | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/LEV_016_page_001.jpg`, `LEV_016_page_002.jpg` | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 102–103 | `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/LEV_016_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `LEV_016_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

*[Note on structure: Leviticus 16 stands as the theological and ritual apex of the entire book — indeed, of the entire Pentateuchal sacrificial system. It is the only chapter that legislates the Yom ha-Kippurim [יוֹם הַכִּפֻּרִים], the Day of Atonements (v.30), the single annual day on which the High Priest enters the innermost chamber of the Tabernacle — the Qodesh ha-Qodashim [קֹדֶשׁ הַקֳּדָשִׁים], Holy of Holies — to make atonement for the sanctuary itself, for the priestly house, and for the entire congregation of Yisra'el. The chapter is introduced with a narrative hook: "after the death of the two sons of Aharon" (v.1), situating the legislation in the aftermath of the catastrophe of ch.10, where Nadab and Abihu died for entering the divine Presence unbidden. That story thus provides the occasion for the legislation that answers the question: when and how may anyone legitimately enter? The answer is: once a year, with specific preparation, specific garments, specific offerings, and specific blood rites. The chapter divides into seven major sections: (1) vv.1–2: introduction and divine prohibition; (2) vv.3–5: what Aharon must bring; (3) vv.6–10: the two goats — the lot-casting ceremony; (4) vv.11–19: the blood rites inside the sanctuary — purging the Holy of Holies, the outer Holy Place, and the altar; (5) vv.20–22: the live Azazel goat — confession and dispatch to the wilderness; (6) vv.23–28: the concluding rites, garment changes, and burning of remains; (7) vv.29–34: the permanent statute — the people's obligations on the tenth day of the seventh month. The two goats are the chapter's most striking ritual element. One goat is slaughtered as a chattat for the people; its blood is brought into the Holy of Holies. The other — the Azazel goat — bears, by the act of Aharon's confessing hand, the iniquities of all Yisra'el and is sent alive into the wilderness. The identity of Azazel [עֲזָאזֵל] is one of the most disputed questions in biblical scholarship (see term table below). The chapter closes with the only occurrence of the doubled formula shabbat shabbaton [שַׁבַּת שַׁבָּתוֹן] applied to a day other than the weekly Sabbath, establishing the Day of Atonements as a festival-Sabbath of the highest category.]*

---

**1** And the LORD [YHWH] spoke [vayedabber] to Moshe [el-Moshe] [el-Moshe — "to Moshe"; unlike Leviticus 15:1, this address is to Moshe alone, not the dual Moshe-and-Aharon formula; Aharon is the subject of the legislation, not its co-recipient; the LORD speaks to Moshe about what Aharon must do; LXX: Καὶ ἐλάλησεν κύριος πρὸς Μωυσῆν (Kai elalēsen kyrios pros Mōysēn, "And the LORD spoke to Moshe")] after the death [acharei mot] [acharei mot — "after the death of"; the phrase places the legislation in time: the death of Nadab and Abihu (10:1–2) provides the occasion and the danger-lesson; the legislation answers: how then may anyone enter? LXX: μετὰ τὸ ἀποθανεῖν (meta to apothanein, "after the dying of")] of the two sons of Aharon [shnei benei Aharon] [shnei benei Aharon — "the two sons of Aharon"; Nadab and Abihu; LXX: δύο υἱοὺς Ααρων (dyo huious Aarōn, "the two sons of Aharon")] — when they drew near [be-qorvat'am] [be-qorvat'am — "in their drawing near / when they approached"; infinitive construct of qarav ("to draw near, to approach") with preposition be- and suffix -am ("their/them"): the fatal approach; LXX: ἐν τῷ προσάγειν αὐτούς (en tō prosagein autous, "in their drawing near")] before the LORD [lifnei YHWH] and died [vayyamutu] [vayyamutu — "and they died"; the narrative memory that grounds the legislation; LXX: ἔναντι κυρίου καὶ ἀπέθανον (enanti kyriou kai apethanon, "before the LORD and they died")]:

**2** And the LORD said [vayomer YHWH] to Moshe [el-Moshe]: "Speak [dabber] to Aharon [el-Aharon] [el-Aharon — "to Aharon"; singular address; the prohibition is directed specifically to the High Priest; LXX: λάλησον Ααρων τῷ ἀδελφῷ σου (lalēson Aarōn tō adelphō sou, "speak to Aharon your brother") — the LXX adds "your brother," identifying the relationship explicitly] your brother [achicha] [achicha — "your brother"; Moshe and Aharon as brothers; LXX includes this] — he shall not come [ve-al-yavo] [ve-al-yavo — "and he shall not come / let him not enter"; al + imperfect as prohibitive: the strongest form of prohibition; LXX: μὴ εἰσπορευέσθω (mē eisporeuesthō, "let him not enter")] at all times [be-khol-et] [be-khol-et — "at all times / at any time"; the prohibition is total and unqualified except for the single day prescribed; LXX: πᾶσαν ὥραν (pasan hōran, "at every hour/at any time")] into the holy place [el-ha-qodesh] [el-ha-qodesh — "into the holy place / into the sanctuary"; ha-qodesh here refers to the inner sanctum, the Holy of Holies (Qodesh ha-Qodashim), as the following phrase makes clear; LXX: εἰς τὸ ἅγιον (eis to hagion, "into the holy place")] within the veil [mi-beit la-parokhet] [mi-beit la-parokhet — "from inside / from within to the veil"; beit ("house, inside") + parokhet [פָּרֹכֶת] = "the curtain, the veil" that separates the Holy of Holies from the Holy Place (cf. Exod 26:31–35); the phrase means: the space behind/inside the veil; LXX: ἐσώτερον τοῦ καταπετάσματος (esōteron tou katapetasmatos, "inside the veil"), using καταπέτασμα (katapetasma, "veil, curtain, screen")] before [el-penei] the kapporet [ha-kapporet] [el-penei ha-kapporet — "before the face of the kapporet / in front of the kapporet"; kapporet [כַּפֹּרֶת] = the golden lid of the ark of the testimony, flanked by the two golden cherubim, the site where the LORD appears and speaks (Exod 25:17–22); from kipper ("to cover, to atone"): the kapporet is both literally the "cover" of the ark and theologically the "atonement-place"; English traditions render it "mercy seat" (Tyndale/Luther's Gnadenstuhl) or "atonement cover"; EMET preserves kapporet untranslated; LXX: ἐπὶ πρόσωπον τοῦ ἱλαστηρίου (epi prosōpon tou hilastēriou, "before the face of the mercy seat"), using ἱλαστήριον (hilastērion, "place of atonement, mercy seat") — the word Paul uses in Rom 3:25 of the crucified Messiah] which is upon the ark [asher al-ha-aron] [asher al-ha-aron — "which is upon the ark"; the kapporet rests on top of the ark of the testimony (Exod 25:17); LXX: ὅ ἐστιν ἐπὶ τῆς κιβωτοῦ (ho estin epi tēs kibōtou, "which is upon the ark"), using κιβωτός (kibōtos, "ark, chest")] — that he not die [ve-lo' yamut] [ve-lo' yamut — "so that he not die"; the purpose of the restriction: unauthorized entry into the divine Presence is fatal; the same fate as Nadab and Abihu; LXX: καὶ οὐκ ἀποθανεῖται (kai ouk apothaneitai, "and he shall not die")] — for [ki] in a cloud [ba-'anan] [ba-'anan — "in a cloud / with a cloud"; the LORD appears at the kapporet within the cloud of the divine Presence; the cloud (anan) is the theophanic marker throughout the Exodus-Leviticus-Numbers narrative (Exod 13:21, 14:19, 40:34–38); LXX: ἐν νεφέλῃ (en nephelē, "in a cloud")] I will appear [era'eh] [era'eh — "I will appear / I appear"; niphal imperfect of ra'ah ("to see"): "I allow myself to be seen / I appear"; YHWH appears at the kapporet — it is the point of theophany; LXX: ὀφθήσομαι (ophthēsomai, "I will be seen / I will appear")] upon the kapporet [al-ha-kapporet]:

**3** With this [be-zot] [be-zot — "in/with this / by means of this"; the demonstrative: "by means of the following" — what is described in vv.3–5 is the required entry-protocol; LXX: οὕτως (houtōs, "thus / in this way")] shall Aharon come [yavo Aharon] [yavo Aharon — "Aharon shall come"; the only permitted access is carefully prescribed; LXX: εἰσελεύσεται Ααρων (eiseleusetai Aarōn, "Aharon shall enter")] into the holy place [el-ha-qodesh] [el-ha-qodesh — the inner sanctuary as in v.2; LXX: εἰς τὸ ἅγιον (eis to hagion, "into the holy")] — with a bull [bi-far par] [bi-far par — "with a young bull / with a bull, a bull"; par = "bull, bullock"; the construct far adds emphasis; the definitive offering for the priestly atonement; LXX: ἐν μόσχῳ ἐκ βοῶν (en moschō ek boōn, "with a calf from cattle"), using μόσχος (moschos, "calf, young bull")] as a sin offering [ben-baqar le-chattat] [ben-baqar le-chattat — "son of the herd / a male of the cattle as a sin offering"; ben-baqar = "young bull, son of the herd"; le-chattat = "for a sin offering"; chattat [חַטָּאת] is the standard purification/sin offering; LXX: περὶ ἁμαρτίας (peri hamartias, "for/concerning sin")] and a ram [ve-ayil] as a burnt offering [le-olah] [ve-ayil le-olah — "and a ram as a burnt offering"; ayil = "ram"; the ram complements the bull; LXX: καὶ κριὸν εἰς ὁλοκαύτωμα (kai krion eis holokautōma, "and a ram for a burnt offering"), using κριός (krios, "ram")]:

**4** A holy linen tunic [kutonet-bad qodesh] [kutonet-bad qodesh — "a tunic of linen, holy / a linen tunic, holy"; kutonet = "tunic, undergarment"; bad = "linen, fine linen, byssus" (specifically this word refers to linen thread/cloth); qodesh as attributive: "holy linen tunic"; the special white linen vestments of the Day of Atonement are described in contrast to the golden vestments of regular high-priestly service (Exod 28); on this day Aharon wears only simple white linen — the garments of service without ornamentation; LXX: χιτῶνα λινοῦν ἡγιασμένον (chitōna linoun hēgiasmenon, "a sanctified linen tunic"), using χιτών (chitōn, "tunic") and λινοῦς (linous, "linen")] shall he wear [yilbash] [yilbash — "he shall put on / he shall wear"; qal imperfect; LXX: ἐνδύσεται (endysetai, "shall put on")] — and linen breeches [u-mikhnesei-bad] [u-mikhnesei-bad — "and breeches of linen / linen underpants"; mikhnesayim (pl.) = "breeches, drawers, underpants"; the inner lower garment; bad = linen; LXX: καὶ περισκελῆ λινᾶ (kai periskelē lina, "and linen breeches"), using περισκελής (periskelēs, "leg-garment, breeches")] shall be [yihyu] upon his flesh [al-besaro] [al-besaro — "upon his flesh / next to his body"; the linen breeches are worn directly on the skin; LXX: ἔσται ἐπὶ τοῦ σώματος αὐτοῦ (estai epi tou sōmatos autou, "shall be upon his body")] — and with a linen sash [u-ve-avnet bad] [u-ve-avnet bad — "and with a sash of linen / and with a linen belt"; avnet = "sash, girdle, belt" — one of the regular high-priestly vestments (Exod 28:4), but normally of colored embroidery; here it must be bad (linen), plain and white; LXX: καὶ ζώνῃ λινῇ (kai zōnē linē, "and with a linen belt")] shall he gird himself [yachgore] [yachgore — "he shall gird himself"; qal imperfect; LXX: ζωσθήσεται (zōsthēsetai, "shall be girded")] — and with a linen turban [u-ve-mitsnefet bad] [u-ve-mitsnefet bad — "and with a turban of linen / and with a linen turban/mitre"; mitsnefet = "turban, head-wrapping, mitre" — the high-priestly head-covering (Exod 28:4, 36–38); again of plain bad/linen rather than the ornamented version; LXX: καὶ κίδαριν λινῆν (kai kidarin linēn, "and a linen turban"), using κίδαρις (kidaris, "turban, mitre")] shall he wrap his head [yitsnof] [yitsnof — "he shall turban / he shall wrap (with a turban)"; denominative from mitsnefet; LXX: περιθήσεται τῇ κεφαλῇ αὐτοῦ (perithēsetai tē kephalē autou, "shall place around his head")] — these are [hem] holy garments [bigdei qodesh] [bigdei qodesh — "garments of holiness / holy vestments"; bigdei = construct plural of beged ("garment"); qodesh ("holiness"): the whole set of four white linen garments constitutes a holy vestment-set; LXX: ἱμάτια ἅγιά ἐστιν (himatia hagia estin, "they are holy garments")] — and he shall bathe [ve-rachatz] his flesh [et-besaro] in water [ba-mayim] and put them on [u-levesham] [u-levesham — "and put them on / and clothe himself in them"; the bathing precedes the donning; LXX: καὶ λούσεται τὸ σῶμα αὐτοῦ ὕδατι καὶ ἐνδύσεται αὐτά (kai loussetai to sōma autou hydati kai endysetai auta, "and shall wash his body in water and put them on")]:

**5** "And from the congregation [u-me'et adat] [u-me'et adat — "and from the congregation of"; the people collectively provide the offering material; LXX: καὶ παρὰ τῆς συναγωγῆς (kai para tēs synagōgēs, "and from the congregation"), using συναγωγή (synagōgē, "congregation, assembly")] of the sons of Yisra'el [benei Yisra'el] he shall take [yiqqach] two male goats [shnei se'irei izzim] [shnei se'irei izzim — "two goats of goats / two male goats"; se'ir = "goat" (specifically a male goat, from sa'ar "hairy"); izzim = "she-goats/goats (generic)"; the pairing: se'irei izzim = "he-goats of the goats"; LXX: δύο χιμάρους ἐξ αἰγῶν (dyo chimarous ex aigōn, "two young male goats from the goats"), using χίμαρος (chimaros, "young goat, he-goat")] as a sin offering [le-chattat] [le-chattat — the two goats together constitute the communal chattat; LXX: περὶ ἁμαρτίας (peri hamartias, "for sin")] and one ram [ve-ayil echad] as a burnt offering [le-olah] [ve-ayil echad le-olah — "and one ram as a burnt offering"; LXX: καὶ κριὸν ἕνα εἰς ὁλοκαύτωμα (kai krion hena eis holokautōma, "and one ram for a burnt offering")]:

**6** "And Aharon shall bring near [ve-hiqriv Aharon] [ve-hiqriv Aharon — "and Aharon shall present / bring near"; hiphil of qarav: "to cause to draw near, to present"; LXX: καὶ προσάξει Ααρων τὸν μόσχον (kai prosexei Aarōn ton moschon, "and Aharon shall bring near the calf")] the bull of the sin offering [et-par ha-chattat] [et-par ha-chattat — "the bull of the sin offering"; par as in v.3; the bull is specifically for the priestly atonement (Aharon's house), not the people's; LXX: τὸν μόσχον τὸν περὶ τῆς ἁμαρτίας (ton moschon ton peri tēs hamartias, "the calf for sin")] which is for himself [asher-lo] [asher-lo — "which is for himself"; the bull is for Aharon's personal atonement (and his household); distinct from the goat which is for the people; LXX: τὸν ἑαυτοῦ (ton heautou, "the one belonging to himself")] — and he shall make atonement [ve-khipper] for himself [ba'ado] [ba'ado — "for himself / on his own behalf"; ba'ad = "on behalf of, for the sake of"; LXX: καὶ ἐξιλάσεται περὶ ἑαυτοῦ (kai exilasetai peri heautou, "and shall make atonement for himself")] and for his household [u-ve'ad beito] [u-ve'ad beito — "and on behalf of his house / for his household"; the entire priestly family; LXX: καὶ τοῦ οἴκου αὐτοῦ (kai tou oikou autou, "and of his household")]:

**7** "And he shall take [ve-laqach] the two goats [et-shnei ha-se'irim] [et-shnei ha-se'irim — "the two goats"; the pair provided by the congregation in v.5; LXX: καὶ λήμψεται τοὺς δύο χιμάρους (kai lēmpsetai tous dyo chimarous, "and shall take the two young goats")] and he shall stand them [ve-he'emid otam] [ve-he'emid otam — "and shall cause them to stand / shall station them"; hiphil of 'amad: "to cause to stand, to set up, to station"; LXX: καὶ στήσει αὐτούς (kai stēsei autous, "and shall stand them")] before the LORD [lifnei YHWH] [lifnei YHWH — "before the LORD / in the presence of the LORD"; the goats are presented before YHWH at the entrance of the tent of meeting before the lot is cast; LXX: ἔναντι κυρίου (enanti kyriou, "before the LORD")] at the entrance of the tent of meeting [petach ohel mo'ed] [petach ohel mo'ed — "at the entrance of the tent of meeting"; the outer court, at the entrance; the lots are cast publicly, in the divine Presence; LXX: παρὰ τὴν θύραν τῆς σκηνῆς τοῦ μαρτυρίου (para tēn thyran tēs skēnēs tou martyriou, "beside the door of the tent of testimony")]:

**8** "And Aharon shall cast [ve-natan Aharon al-shnei ha-se'irim] [ve-natan Aharon al-shnei ha-se'irim — "and Aharon shall give / place upon the two goats"; natan here in the sense of "to place, to assign, to give": he places/assigns lots upon the two goats; LXX: καὶ ἐπιθήσει Ααρων ἐπὶ τοὺς δύο χιμάρους (kai epithēsei Aarōn epi tous dyo chimarous, "and Aharon shall place upon the two young goats")] lots [goralot] [goralot [גּוֹרָלוֹת] — "lots"; goral [גּוֹרָל] (pl. goralot) = "lot, portion assigned by lot"; the physical object used in lot-casting was typically a stone, potsherd, or wooden stick, drawn from a vessel or thrown; the lot determined YHWH's decision, removing human choice; LXX: κλήρους (klērous, "lots"), using κλῆρος (klēros, "lot, allotment, portion")] — one lot [goral echad] for the LORD [la-YHWH] [la-YHWH — "for YHWH / belonging to the LORD"; the lot assigns this goat to be offered as a sacrifice before YHWH; LXX: ἕνα τῷ κυρίῳ (hena tō kyriō, "one to the LORD")] and one lot [ve-goral echad] for Azazel [la-Azazel] [la-Azazel — "for Azazel / belonging to Azazel"; Azazel [עֲזָאזֵל] is one of the most contested terms in the entire Hebrew Bible; see term table below for full discussion; the grammatical structure perfectly parallels la-YHWH: whatever Azazel is, it receives the lot-assigned goat in a manner that grammatically mirrors YHWH receiving the other; LXX: ἕνα τῷ ἀποπομπαίῳ (hena tō apompaiō, "one for the Apopompaion/one for the Averter"), using ἀποπομπαῖος (apompaios, "one who sends away, averter, one who removes evil") — the LXX renders Azazel as a concept of removal/averting rather than a personal name]:

**9** "And Aharon shall bring near [ve-hiqriv Aharon] the goat [et-ha-sa'ir] [et-ha-sa'ir — "the goat"; the definite article: the specific goat upon which the LORD's lot fell; LXX: καὶ προσάξει Ααρων τὸν χίμαρον (kai prosexei Aarōn ton chimaron, "and Aharon shall bring near the young goat")] upon which fell [asher alah alav] [asher alah alav — "upon which came up / upon which went up"; alah = "to go up, to come up, to ascend"; the lot "comes up / falls" on the goat; the imagery may be of drawing from a vessel (the lot comes up when drawn); LXX: οὗ ἐπέβη ὁ κλῆρος (hou epebē ho klēros, "upon which the lot fell"), using ἐπιβαίνω (epibainō, "to step upon, to fall upon")] the lot [ha-goral] for the LORD [la-YHWH] [la-YHWH — as in v.8; LXX: τῷ κυρίῳ (tō kyriō, "for the LORD")] and he shall make it [ve-'asahu] [ve-'asahu — "and shall make it / shall do/perform with it"; 'asah in the cultic sense: "to perform, to offer"; LXX: καὶ προσοίσει αὐτόν (kai prosoisei auton, "and shall present/bring it")] as a sin offering [chattat] [chattat — the goat for YHWH is the chattat for the people; its blood is brought inside the veil; LXX: περὶ ἁμαρτίας (peri hamartias, "for sin")]:

**10** "And the goat [ve-ha-sa'ir] upon which fell [asher alah alav] the lot [ha-goral] for Azazel [la-Azazel] [la-Azazel — as in v.8; LXX: ὁ δὲ χίμαρος ἐφ' ὃν ἐπέβη ὁ κλῆρος τοῦ ἀποπομπαίου (ho de chimaros eph' hon epebē ho klēros tou apompaiou, "and the young goat upon which the lot of the Apopompaion fell")] shall be stood [yu'amad] [yu'amad — "shall be caused to stand / shall be stationed"; hophal of 'amad: passive: "shall be stood up, placed in position"; the live goat is kept alive and standing while the rite proceeds; LXX: σταθήσεται (stathēsetai, "shall be set, shall be stood")] alive [chai] [chai — "living / alive"; the Azazel goat is not slaughtered but kept alive throughout the ceremony; LXX: ζῶν (zōn, "living")] before the LORD [lifnei YHWH] [lifnei YHWH — the live goat is presented before YHWH before the confession is made; LXX: ζῶν ἔναντι κυρίου (zōn enanti kyriou, "living before the LORD")] to make atonement over it [le-khapper alav] [le-khapper alav — "to make atonement over/upon it / to make expiation by means of it"; le-khapper = infinitive construct of kipper: "for the purpose of making atonement"; alav = "upon it / over it"; the live goat is the vehicle of the atonement-act of confession and sending; LXX: ὥστε ἐξιλάσασθαι ἐπ' αὐτοῦ (hōste exilasasthai ep' autou, "so as to make atonement over/upon it")] — to send it away [le-shallach oto] [le-shallach oto — "to send it away / to dispatch it"; piel infinitive of shalach: "to send away, to release, to dispatch"; the live goat is ultimately sent away into the wilderness; LXX: ὥστε ἀποστεῖλαι αὐτόν (hōste aposteilai auton, "so as to send it away")] as Azazel [la-Azazel] [la-Azazel — the second Azazel-reference; the preposition la- ("for, to, toward") is repeated; the goat is sent "as/to/for Azazel"; LXX: εἰς τὴν ἀποπομπήν (eis tēn apompēn, "into the sending-away"), using ἀποπομπή (apompē, "a sending away, dismissal, ritual removal")] to the wilderness [ha-midbarah] [ha-midbarah — "to the wilderness / into the wilderness"; the definite article with directional he suffix (-ah): "toward/into the wilderness"; LXX: εἰς τὴν ἔρημον (eis tēn erēmon, "into the wilderness"), using ἔρημος (erēmos, "wilderness, desert")]:

---

**11** "And Aharon shall bring near [ve-hiqriv Aharon] the bull of the sin offering [et-par ha-chattat] [et-par ha-chattat — "the bull of the sin offering"; as in v.6; the same bull presented in v.3; LXX: τὸν μόσχον τὸν περὶ τῆς ἁμαρτίας (ton moschon ton peri tēs hamartias, "the calf for sin")] which is for himself [asher-lo] [asher-lo — "which is for himself"; the priestly chattat; LXX: τὸν ἑαυτοῦ (ton heautou, "the one for himself")] and shall make atonement [ve-khipper] for himself [ba'ado] and for his household [u-ve'ad beito] [ba'ado u-ve'ad beito — as in v.6; the formulaic repetition is exact; LXX: καὶ ἐξιλάσεται περὶ ἑαυτοῦ καὶ τοῦ οἴκου αὐτοῦ (kai exilasetai peri heautou kai tou oikou autou, "and shall make atonement for himself and for his household")] — and he shall slaughter [ve-shachat] [ve-shachat — "and he shall slaughter"; qal perfect or imperfect (apocopated): the slaughter; this is the first time slaughter is mentioned — vv.6 and 11 both describe bringing the bull near and making atonement; v.11 now moves to the slaughter; LXX: καὶ σφάξει (kai sphaxei, "and shall slaughter"), using σφάζω (sphazō, "to slaughter, to sacrifice")] the bull of the sin offering which is for himself [et-par ha-chattat asher-lo]:

**12** "And he shall take [ve-laqach] [ve-laqach — "and he shall take"; the censing procedure begins; LXX: καὶ λήμψεται (kai lēmpsetai, "and shall take")] a censer full [melo' ha-machtah] [melo' ha-machtah — "fullness of the censer / a censer full"; melo' = "fulness of / full of"; machtah = "fire-pan, censer, pan of coals"; the censer is filled; LXX: τὸ πυρεῖον πλῆρες (to pyreion plēres, "the fire-pan full"), using πυρεῖον (pyreion, "fire-pan, censer, brazier")] of fiery coals [gachalei esh] [gachalei esh — "coals of fire / burning coals"; gachal (pl. gachalim) = "glowing coal, coal"; esh = "fire"; LXX: ἀνθράκων πυρός (anthrākōn pyros, "coals of fire"), using ἄνθραξ (anthrax, "charcoal, coal")] from upon the altar [me'al ha-mizbeath] [me'al ha-mizbeath — "from upon the altar"; the altar of burnt offering in the outer court; LXX: ἀπὸ τοῦ θυσιαστηρίου (apo tou thysiastēriou, "from the altar")] before the LORD [mi-lifnei YHWH] [mi-lifnei YHWH — "from before the LORD / from the presence of the LORD"; the coals come from the divine altar-fire; LXX: ἀπὸ προσώπου κυρίου (apo prosōpou kyriou, "from before the face of the LORD")] — and his hands full [u-melo' chaphnav] [u-melo' chaphnav — "and the fulness of his hands / both hands full"; chaphnav = "his (two) fists/handfuls"; melo' chaphnav = "a double-handful, two handfuls"; the incense is carried in cupped hands; LXX: καὶ τὰς χεῖρας πλήρεις (kai tas cheiras plēreis, "and the hands full")] of fine incense spices [qetoret sammim] [qetoret sammim — "incense of spices / spiced incense"; qetoret [קְטֹרֶת] = "incense, smoke, fragrant offering" (from qatar, "to burn offering/incense"); sammim = "spices, aromatic drugs"; the sacred incense of Exod 30:34–38; LXX: θυμιάματος λεπτοῦ (thymiamatos leptou, "fine/thin incense"), using θυμίαμα (thymiama, "incense, sacrifice of incense") and λεπτός (leptos, "fine, thin, subtle")] ground small [daqah] [daqah — "ground fine / beaten fine, powdered"; feminine adjective qualifying qetoret: "fine incense"; LXX: λεπτοῦ already; some MSS add τριτοῦ (tritou, "thrice-ground")] — and he shall bring [ve-hevi'] [ve-hevi' — "and shall bring"; hiphil of bo': "to bring, to cause to come"; LXX: καὶ εἰσοίσει (kai eisoisei, "and shall bring in")] them [otam] from within [mi-beit] to the veil [la-parokhet] [mi-beit la-parokhet — "from inside to the veil / beyond the veil"; as in v.2: the space behind the veil, the Holy of Holies; LXX: ἐσώτερον τοῦ καταπετάσματος (esōteron tou katapetasmatos, "inside the veil")]:

**13** "And he shall put [ve-natan] [ve-natan — "and shall put / shall place"; natan: to place, to set; LXX: καὶ ἐπιθήσει (kai epithēsei, "and shall place upon")] the incense [et-ha-qetoret] upon the fire [al-ha-esh] [al-ha-esh — "upon the fire"; the incense is placed on the coals in the censer inside the Holy of Holies; LXX: τὸ θυμίαμα ἐπὶ τὸ πῦρ (to thymiama epi to pyr, "the incense upon the fire")] before the LORD [lifnei YHWH] [lifnei YHWH — the incense is burned in the divine Presence; LXX: ἔναντι κυρίου (enanti kyriou, "before the LORD")] — and the cloud of the incense [va-khissah anan ha-qetoret] [va-khissah anan ha-qetoret — "and the cloud of the incense shall cover"; khissah = piel perfect/imperfect of kassah ("to cover, to conceal, to overwhelm"): "the cloud of incense covers / shall cover"; anan ha-qetoret = "cloud of the incense"; the incense-cloud serves as a visual screen between the High Priest and the divine Presence on the kapporet; LXX: καὶ καλύψει ἡ νεφέλη τοῦ θυμιάματος (kai kalypsei hē nephelē tou thymiamatos, "and the cloud of the incense shall cover")] shall cover the kapporet [et-ha-kapporet] [et-ha-kapporet — "the kapporet / the atonement cover"; the cloud of incense screens the divine Presence on the kapporet; LXX: τὸ ἱλαστήριον (to hilastērion, "the mercy seat")] which is upon the testimony [asher al-ha-edut] [asher al-ha-edut — "which is upon the testimony / upon the covenant tablets"; ha-edut = "the testimony, the covenant"; the ark is called aron ha-edut, "the ark of the testimony" (Exod 25:22), because it contains the tablets of the covenant; the kapporet rests upon this ark; LXX: ὅ ἐστιν ἐπὶ τῶν μαρτυρίων (ho estin epi tōn martyriōn, "which is upon the testimonies")] — so that he not die [ve-lo' yamut] [ve-lo' yamut — "so that he not die"; the incense-cloud is not ornamental — it is a life-protecting screen; without the screen the divine Presence destroys the one who approaches; LXX: καὶ οὐκ ἀποθανεῖται (kai ouk apothaneitai, "and he shall not die")]:

**14** "And he shall take [ve-laqach] some of the blood of the bull [mi-dam ha-par] [mi-dam ha-par — "from the blood of the bull"; the partitive min: "some of the blood"; the bull has now been slaughtered (v.11); its blood is taken inside the veil; LXX: καὶ λήμψεται ἀπὸ τοῦ αἵματος τοῦ μόσχου (kai lēmpsetai apo tou haimatos tou moschon, "and shall take from the blood of the calf")] and shall sprinkle [ve-hizzah] [ve-hizzah — "and shall sprinkle"; hiphil of nazzah: "to cause to sprinkle, to sprinkle"; the technical term for blood-sprinkling in the sanctuary rite; LXX: καὶ ῥανεῖ (kai ranei, "and shall sprinkle"), using ῥαίνω (rainō, "to sprinkle, to besprinkle")] with his finger [be-etzba'o] [be-etzba'o — "with his finger"; etzba' = "finger"; the application of blood is done with the finger, not an instrument; LXX: τῷ δακτύλῳ αὐτοῦ (tō daktylō autou, "with his finger")] upon the face of the kapporet [al-penei ha-kapporet] [al-penei ha-kapporet — "upon the face of the kapporet / on the front of the atonement cover"; the upper surface of the kapporet, facing eastward; LXX: ἐπὶ τὸ ἱλαστήριον κατὰ ἀνατολάς (epi to hilastērion kata anatolasi, "upon the mercy seat toward the east") — the LXX specifies "toward the east" here] eastward [qedmah] [qedmah — "eastward / toward the east"; the directional suffix -ah: "in the direction of east"; the sprinkling is done on the east side (front face) of the kapporet; LXX: ἀπέναντι τοῦ ἱλαστηρίου (apenanti tou hilastēriou, "in front of the mercy seat")] — and before [u-lifnei] the kapporet [ha-kapporet] he shall sprinkle seven times [yazzeh sheva-pe'amim] [yazzeh sheva-pe'amim — "shall sprinkle seven times"; the seven-fold sprinkling before (in front of) the kapporet is distinct from the single sprinkling upon (al) its face; together: one spray upon the top, seven before it; LXX: καὶ ἀπέναντι τοῦ ἱλαστηρίου ῥανεῖ ἑπτάκις (kai apenanti tou hilastēriou ranei heptakis, "and before the mercy seat shall sprinkle seven times")] some of the blood [min-ha-dam] [min-ha-dam — "from the blood"; the same partitive as above; LXX: ἀπὸ τοῦ αἵματος (apo tou haimatos, "from the blood")] with his finger [be-etzba'o]:

**15** "And he shall slaughter [ve-shachat] [ve-shachat — "and he shall slaughter"; as in v.11; LXX: καὶ σφάξει (kai sphaxei, "and shall slaughter")] the goat of the sin offering [et-se'ir ha-chattat] [et-se'ir ha-chattat — "the goat of the sin offering"; the YHWH-lot goat of v.9; LXX: τὸν χίμαρον τὸν περὶ τῆς ἁμαρτίας (ton chimaron ton peri tēs hamartias, "the young goat for sin")] which is for the people [asher la-am] [asher la-am — "which is for the people / which belongs to the people"; the goat-chattat is for the communal atonement; LXX: τὸν περὶ τῆς ἁμαρτίας τὸν περὶ τοῦ λαοῦ (ton peri tēs hamartias ton peri tou laou, "the one for sin, the one for the people")] and he shall bring [ve-hevi'] its blood [et-damo] [et-damo — "its blood"; the goat's blood; LXX: καὶ εἰσοίσει τοῦ αἵματος αὐτοῦ (kai eisoisei tou haimatos autou, "and shall bring in of its blood")] from within [el-mi-beit] to the veil [la-parokhet] [el-mi-beit la-parokhet — "inside/beyond the veil"; the same formula as v.12; LXX: ἐσώτερον τοῦ καταπετάσματος (esōteron tou katapetasmatos, "inside the veil")] — and he shall do [ve-'asah] [ve-'asah — "and he shall do"; 'asah: "to perform, to do, to make"; LXX: καὶ ποιήσει (kai poiēsei, "and shall make/do")] with its blood [et-damo] as he did [ka-asher asah] with the blood of the bull [le-dam ha-par] [le-dam ha-par — "to/with the blood of the bull"; the goat's blood is treated exactly as the bull's blood (v.14): sprinkled upon and before the kapporet; LXX: καθάπερ ἐποίησεν τὸ αἷμα τοῦ μόσχου (kathaper epoiēsen to haima tou moschon, "just as he did with the blood of the calf")] and shall sprinkle it [ve-hizzah oto] upon the face of the kapporet [al-penei ha-kapporet] [al-penei ha-kapporet — as in v.14; LXX: καὶ ῥανεῖ αὐτὸ ἐπὶ τὸ ἱλαστήριον (kai ranei auto epi to hilastērion, "and shall sprinkle it upon the mercy seat")] and before the kapporet [u-lifnei ha-kapporet]:

**16** "And he shall make atonement [ve-khipper] for the holy place [al-ha-qodesh] [al-ha-qodesh — "for the holy place / over the sanctuary"; the atonement is made for the sanctuary itself, which has been polluted by the impurities of Yisra'el; this is a crucial theological point: the sanctuary requires atonement not because it sinned, but because Israel's sin and impurity contaminated it; LXX: καὶ ἐξιλάσεται τὸ ἅγιον (kai exilasetai to hagion, "and shall make atonement for the holy")] from [mi-] the uncleannesses [tum'ot] [tum'ot — "uncleannesses / impurities"; plural; the breadth of Israel's ritual impurity; LXX: ἀπὸ τῶν ἀκαθαρσιῶν (apo tōn akatharsion, "from the impurities")] of the sons of Yisra'el [benei Yisra'el] and from [u-mi-] their transgressions [pish'eihem] [pish'eihem — "their transgressions / their rebellions"; pesha' [פֶּשַׁע] = "transgression, rebellion, revolt" — the most politically charged of the three sin-vocabulary words: it implies willful defection, revolt against authority; LXX: ἀπὸ τῶν ἀδικιῶν αὐτῶν (apo tōn adikiōn autōn, "from their unrighteous acts")] for all their sins [le-khol-chatto'tam] [le-khol-chatto'tam — "for all their sins"; chatta'ah [חַטָּאָה] = "sin, sin-condition, missing"; the three terms tum'ot, pish'eihem, and chatto'tam represent three distinct categories: ritual impurity, willful rebellion, and individual sin; together they cover the entire moral and ritual spectrum; LXX: καὶ περὶ πασῶν τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν αὐτῶν (kai peri pasōn tōn hamartiōn autōn, "and for all their sins")] — and so he shall do [ve-khen ya'aseh] [ve-khen ya'aseh — "and so he shall do / and thus he shall perform it"; the formula applies to the Tent of Meeting below; LXX: καὶ οὕτως ποιήσει (kai houtōs poiēsei, "and so shall he do")] for the tent of meeting [le-ohel mo'ed] [le-ohel mo'ed — "for the tent of meeting"; the broader sanctuary complex (not just the Holy of Holies); LXX: τῇ σκηνῇ τοῦ μαρτυρίου (tē skēnē tou martyriou, "the tent of testimony")] that dwells [ha-shokhein] [ha-shokhein — "the one dwelling / which dwells"; qal participle of shakhan ("to dwell, to tabernacle"): the Tabernacle as the LORD's dwelling place; LXX: τῆς κατεσκηνωκυίας (tēs kateskēnōkyias, "the one having tabernacled / which dwells")] with them [ittam] [ittam — "with them / in their midst"; the dangerous intimacy of the divine dwelling: YHWH dwells among Israel, making both blessing and deadly contamination possible; LXX: μετ' αὐτῶν (met' autōn, "with them")] in the midst of their uncleannesses [be-tokh tum'otam] [be-tokh tum'otam — "in the midst of their impurities"; tokh = "midst, interior"; the Tabernacle dwells in the midst of an impure people — this is both the theological problem and the reason for the annual Day of Atonement rite; LXX: ἐν μέσῳ τῆς ἀκαθαρσίας αὐτῶν (en mesō tēs akatharsias autōn, "in the midst of their impurity")]:

**17** "And no man [ve-khol-adam lo'] [ve-khol-adam lo' — "and no man / and any man not"; the total exclusion; LXX: καὶ πᾶς ἄνθρωπος οὐκ ἔσται (kai pas anthrōpos ouk estai, "and no man shall be")] shall be in the tent of meeting [bi-ohel mo'ed] [bi-ohel mo'ed — "in the tent of meeting"; LXX: ἐν τῇ σκηνῇ τοῦ μαρτυρίου (en tē skēnē tou martyriou, "in the tent of testimony")] when he comes in [be-vo'o] [be-vo'o — "in his coming in / when he enters"; infinitive construct of bo' with possessive suffix; LXX: ὅταν εἰσέλθῃ (hotan eiselthē, "whenever he enters")] to make atonement [le-khapper] in the holy place [ba-qodesh] [le-khapper ba-qodesh — "to make atonement in the holy place"; LXX: ἐξιλάσκεσθαι ἐν τῷ ἁγίῳ (exilaskesthai en tō hagiō, "to make atonement in the holy")] until he comes out [ad-tzet'o] [ad-tzet'o — "until his going out / until he comes out"; infinitive construct of yatza' with possessive; LXX: ἕως ἂν ἐξέλθῃ (heōs an exelthē, "until he goes out")] — and he shall make atonement [ve-khipper] for himself [ba'ado] [ba'ado — as in v.6, 11; LXX: καὶ ἐξιλάσεται (kai exilasetai, "and shall make atonement")] and for his household [u-ve'ad beito] [u-ve'ad beito — as in v.6, 11; LXX: περὶ ἑαυτοῦ καὶ τοῦ οἴκου αὐτοῦ (peri heautou kai tou oikou autou, "for himself and for his household")] and for all the congregation of Yisra'el [u-ve'ad kol-qehal Yisra'el] [u-ve'ad kol-qehal Yisra'el — "and on behalf of all the assembly of Yisra'el"; qehal [קְהַל] = "assembly, congregation, gathered community" (often used of the full assembly gathered for a specific event); LXX: καὶ τῆς συναγωγῆς πάσης υἱῶν Ισραηλ (kai tēs synagōgēs pasēs huiōn Israēl, "and for the whole congregation of the sons of Israel")]:

**18** "And he shall go out [ve-yatza'] [ve-yatza' — "and he shall go out"; he exits the Holy of Holies to the altar in the outer court; LXX: καὶ ἐξελεύσεται (kai exeleusetai, "and shall go out")] to the altar [el-ha-mizbeath] [el-ha-mizbeath — "to the altar"; the altar of burnt offering in the outer court; LXX: ἐπὶ τὸ θυσιαστήριον τὸ ἔμπροσθεν κυρίου (epi to thysiastērion to emprosthen kyriou, "to the altar before the LORD")] which is before the LORD [asher lifnei YHWH] [asher lifnei YHWH — "which is before the LORD"; the outer altar; LXX: ἐπὶ τὸ θυσιαστήριον (epi to thysiastērion, "to the altar")] and he shall make atonement for it [ve-khipper alav] [ve-khipper alav — "and shall make atonement over it / for it"; alav = "upon it / over it / for it"; the altar itself requires atonement-purging; LXX: καὶ ἐξιλάσεται ἐπ' αὐτό (kai exilasetai ep' auto, "and shall make atonement for it")] — and he shall take [ve-laqach] some of the blood of the bull [mi-dam ha-par] [mi-dam ha-par — as in v.14; LXX: καὶ λήμψεται ἀπὸ τοῦ αἵματος τοῦ μόσχου (kai lēmpsetai apo tou haimatos tou moschon)] and some of the blood of the goat [u-mi-dam ha-sa'ir] [u-mi-dam ha-sa'ir — "and from the blood of the goat"; the mixture or combined application of both bloods; LXX: καὶ ἀπὸ τοῦ αἵματος τοῦ χιμάρου (kai apo tou haimatos tou chimarou, "and from the blood of the young goat")] and he shall put it [ve-natan] [ve-natan — "and shall give / shall apply"; LXX: καὶ ἐπιθήσει (kai epithēsei, "and shall place upon")] upon the horns [al-qarnot] of the altar [ha-mizbeath] [al-qarnot ha-mizbeath — "upon the horns of the altar"; the four projecting corners of the altar; blood is applied to the horns as in the regular chattat rite (4:7, 4:18, etc.); LXX: ἐπὶ τὰ κέρατα τοῦ θυσιαστηρίου (epi ta kerata tou thysiastēriou, "upon the horns of the altar")] all around [saviv] [saviv — "around, all around, on all sides"; all four horns; LXX: κύκλῳ (kyklō, "in a circle, around")]:

**19** "And he shall sprinkle [ve-hizzah] [ve-hizzah — "and shall sprinkle"; as in v.14; LXX: καὶ ῥανεῖ (kai ranei, "and shall sprinkle")] upon it [alav] some of the blood [min-ha-dam] [min-ha-dam — the blood from the censer; LXX: ἐπ' αὐτοῦ ἀπὸ τοῦ αἵματος (ep' autou apo tou haimatos, "upon it from the blood")] with his finger [be-etzba'o] [be-etzba'o — as in v.14; LXX: τῷ δακτύλῳ αὐτοῦ (tō daktylō autou, "with his finger")] seven times [sheva-pe'amim] [sheva-pe'amim — seven-fold application as at the kapporet; LXX: ἑπτάκις (heptakis, "seven times")] — and he shall cleanse it [ve-tihar'o] [ve-tihar'o — "and shall cleanse it / shall purify it"; piel of taher: "to cleanse, to purify, to declare clean"; the altar is purified from the contamination it has absorbed; LXX: καὶ καθαριεῖ αὐτό (kai katharirei auto, "and shall cleanse it")] and shall consecrate it [ve-qiddesh'o] [ve-qiddesh'o — "and shall consecrate it / shall sanctify it"; piel of qadash: "to make holy, to consecrate, to sanctify"; after purification, the altar is re-consecrated; LXX: καὶ ἁγιάσει αὐτό (kai hagiasi auto, "and shall sanctify it")] from [mi-] the uncleannesses [tum'ot] of the sons of Yisra'el [benei Yisra'el] [mi-tum'ot benei Yisra'el — the source of the altar's contamination is the accumulated impurity of the people; LXX: ἀπὸ τῶν ἀκαθαρσιῶν τῶν υἱῶν Ισραηλ (apo tōn akatharsion tōn huiōn Israēl, "from the impurities of the sons of Israel")]:

---

**20** "And when he finishes [ve-khillah] [ve-khillah — "and when he finishes / and he has completed"; piel of kalah: "to finish, to complete"; the completion marker transitions from the blood rites to the Azazel goat; LXX: καὶ συντελέσει (kai syntelesi, "and shall complete")] making atonement [me-khapper] for the holy place [et-ha-qodesh] [et-ha-qodesh — "the holy place / the sanctuary"; LXX: ἐξιλασμὸν τοῦ ἁγίου (exilasmon tou hagiou, "the atonement of the holy")] and the tent of meeting [ve-ohel mo'ed] [ve-ohel mo'ed — "and the tent of meeting"; LXX: καὶ τῆς σκηνῆς τοῦ μαρτυρίου (kai tēs skēnēs tou martyriou, "and the tent of testimony")] and the altar [ve-ha-mizbeath] [ve-ha-mizbeath — "and the altar"; the threefold sanctuary complex has been purged: (1) Holy of Holies, (2) Holy Place/Tent of Meeting, (3) Outer Altar; LXX: καὶ τοῦ θυσιαστηρίου (kai tou thysiastēriou, "and the altar")] — he shall bring near [ve-hiqriv] [ve-hiqriv — "and he shall bring near / shall present"; LXX: προσάξει (prosexei, "shall bring near")] the live goat [et-ha-sa'ir he-chai] [et-ha-sa'ir he-chai — "the living goat / the live goat"; the Azazel goat kept alive since v.10; LXX: τὸν χίμαρον τὸν ζῶντα (ton chimaron ton zōnta, "the living young goat")]:

**21** "And Aharon shall lay [ve-samakh] [ve-samakh — "and shall press / shall lay"; qal of samakh: "to lean upon, to press upon, to lay (hands) upon"; the technical term for the laying-on of hands in sacrifice; LXX: καὶ ἐπιθήσει Ααρων (kai epithēsei Aarōn, "and Aharon shall place/lay")] both his hands [et-shtei yadav] [et-shtei yadav — "both his hands / his two hands"; shtei = "two"; the use of both hands is significant — it is the full-weight laying, not a partial laying; LXX: ἀμφοτέρας τὰς χεῖρας αὐτοῦ (amphoteras tas cheiras autou, "both his hands")] upon the head of the live goat [al-rosh ha-sa'ir he-chai] [al-rosh ha-sa'ir he-chai — "upon the head of the living goat"; the head is the point of transfer; the confession/sin is transmitted from Aharon's hands through the goat's head into the goat; LXX: ἐπὶ τὴν κεφαλὴν τοῦ χιμάρου τοῦ ζῶντος (epi tēn kephalēn tou chimarou tou zōntos, "upon the head of the living young goat")] and he shall confess [ve-hitvaddah] [ve-hitvaddah — "and he shall confess"; hithpa'el of yadah: reflexive-intensive: "shall acknowledge-upon-himself, shall confess"; hitvaddah [הִתְוַדָּה] is the classic Hebrew term for confession; the hitpa'el form indicates the deep personal engagement of the act; LXX: καὶ ἐξαγορεύσει (kai exagoreusei, "and shall declare/confess"), using ἐξαγορεύω (exagoreuō, "to confess, to declare openly")] over it [alav] [alav — "upon it / over it"; the confession is directed over the goat's head, onto which the hands are pressed; LXX: ἐπ' αὐτοῦ (ep' autou, "upon it")] all the iniquities [et-kol-avonot] [et-kol-avonot — "all the iniquities"; avon [עָוֹן] = "iniquity, perversity, guilt, crookedness"; the deepest of the three sin-words (pesha' = rebellion, chatta'ah = sin, avon = iniquity/guilt); LXX: πάσας τὰς ἀνομίας (pasas tas anomias, "all the lawlessnesses")] of the sons of Yisra'el [benei Yisra'el] and all their transgressions [ve-et-kol-pish'eihem] [ve-et-kol-pish'eihem — "and all their transgressions"; pesha' as in v.16; LXX: καὶ πάσας τὰς ἀδικίας αὐτῶν (kai pasas tas adikias autōn, "and all their unrighteous acts")] for all their sins [le-khol-chatto'tam] [le-khol-chatto'tam — "for all their sins"; the same tripartite formula as v.16: avonot (iniquities), pish'im (transgressions), chatto'ot (sins) — the entire spectrum; LXX: καὶ πάσας τὰς ἁμαρτίας αὐτῶν (kai pasas tas hamartias autōn, "and all their sins")] — and he shall put them [ve-natan otam] [ve-natan otam — "and shall give / put them"; the act of putting the sins onto the goat; LXX: καὶ ἐπιθήσει αὐτὰς (kai epithēsei autas, "and shall place them")] upon the head of the goat [al-rosh ha-sa'ir] [al-rosh ha-sa'ir — "upon the head of the goat"; the head as the locus of symbolic sin-transference; LXX: ἐπὶ τὴν κεφαλὴν τοῦ χιμάρου (epi tēn kephalēn tou chimarou, "upon the head of the young goat")] and he shall send it away [ve-shillach be-yad ish itti] [ve-shillach be-yad ish itti — "and shall send it by the hand of an appointed/prepared man"; itti [עִתִּי] = "the timely one / the one designated for the time / the ready one / the prepared man"; itti from et ("time"): "one pertaining to the time, one prepared for the occasion"; the man designated to lead the goat out; LXX: καὶ ἀποστελεῖ αὐτὸν διὰ χειρὸς ἀνθρώπου ἑτοίμου (kai apostelei auton dia cheiros anthrōpou hetoimou, "and shall send it by the hand of a prepared/ready man"), using ἕτοιμος (hetoimos, "prepared, ready")] into the wilderness [ha-midbarah] [ha-midbarah — "into the wilderness"; as in v.10; LXX: εἰς τὴν ἔρημον (eis tēn erēmon, "into the wilderness")]:

**22** "And the goat shall bear [ve-nasa' ha-sa'ir] [ve-nasa' ha-sa'ir — "and the goat shall carry / shall bear"; nasa' qal: "to lift, to carry, to bear"; the goat as burden-bearer, carrying Israel's sins; LXX: καὶ λήμψεται ὁ χίμαρος (kai lēmpsetai ho chimaros, "and the young goat shall receive/take"), using lambano rather than the carrying verb — a LXX interpretive choice] upon itself [alav] [alav — "upon itself / upon him"; LXX: ἐφ' ἑαυτοῦ (eph' heautou, "upon himself")] all their iniquities [et-kol-avonotam] [et-kol-avonotam — "all their iniquities"; the avonot of v.21; LXX: πάσας τὰς ἀνομίας αὐτῶν (pasas tas anomias autōn, "all their lawlessnesses")] to a land cut off [el-eretz gezarah] [el-eretz gezarah — "to a land of cutting off / to a cut-off land / to an uninhabited land"; gezarah [גְּזֵרָה] from gazar ("to cut, to cut off, to separate"): "a cut-off place / a separated land / an inaccessible region"; the wilderness as a place of total separation, isolation, beyond the inhabited world; this is not a specific place-name but a category; LXX: εἰς γῆν ἄβατον (eis gēn abaton, "into an impassable/trackless land"), using ἄβατος (abatos, "impassable, trackless, desolate") — the LXX beautifully captures the idea of a land that cannot be traversed, utterly remote] — and he shall release [ve-shillach] [ve-shillach — "and he shall release / shall send away"; piel of shalach: "to send away, to release"; the final dispatch; LXX: καὶ ἀποστελεῖ (kai apostelei, "and shall send away")] the goat [et-ha-sa'ir] into the wilderness [ba-midbar] [ba-midbar — "into the wilderness"; LXX: τὸν χίμαρον εἰς τὴν ἔρημον (ton chimaron eis tēn erēmon, "the young goat into the wilderness")]:

---

**23** "And Aharon shall come [u-va Aharon] [u-va Aharon — "and Aharon shall come / shall enter"; LXX: καὶ εἰσελεύσεται Ααρων (kai eiseleusetai Aarōn, "and Aharon shall enter")] into the tent of meeting [el-ohel mo'ed] [el-ohel mo'ed — "into the tent of meeting"; LXX: εἰς τὴν σκηνὴν τοῦ μαρτυρίου (eis tēn skēnēn tou martyriou, "into the tent of testimony")] and he shall strip off [u-fashat] [u-fashat — "and he shall strip off / remove"; qal of pashat ("to strip off, to remove, to peel away"): the removal of the linen vestments; LXX: καὶ ἐκδύσεται (kai ekdysetai, "and shall take off")] the linen garments [et-bigdei ha-bad] [et-bigdei ha-bad — "the linen garments"; the four white linen vestments of vv.4; LXX: τὴν στολὴν τὴν λινῆν (tēn stolēn tēn linēn, "the linen robe/vestment")] which he put on [asher lavash] [asher lavash — "which he put on / which he wore"; lavash qal: "to clothe, to put on, to wear"; LXX: ἣν ἐνεδύσατο (hēn endysato, "which he put on")] when he came into the holy place [be-vo'o el-ha-qodesh] [be-vo'o el-ha-qodesh — "in his coming into the holy place"; the entry described in v.3; LXX: εἰσπορευομένου αὐτοῦ εἰς τὸ ἅγιον (eisporeuomenou autou eis to hagion, "of him entering into the holy")] — and he shall leave them [ve-hinniacham] [ve-hinniacham — "and shall leave them / shall set them down there"; hiphil of nuach: "to cause to rest, to deposit, to lay down"; the linen garments are set down in the Holy Place after the rite; LXX: καὶ ἀποθήσεται αὐτά (kai apothēsetai auta, "and shall lay them aside")] there [sham] [sham — "there"; in the Holy Place; LXX: ἐκεῖ (ekei, "there")]:

**24** "And he shall bathe [ve-rachatz] his flesh [et-besaro] [et-besaro — "his flesh / his body"; LXX: καὶ λούσεται τὸ σῶμα αὐτοῦ (kai loussetai to sōma autou, "and shall wash his body")] in water [ba-mayim] [ba-mayim — "in water"; LXX: ὕδατι (hydati, "in water")] in the holy place [ba-maqom ha-qadosh] [ba-maqom ha-qadosh — "in the holy place / in a holy location"; maqom qadosh = "holy place"; the bathing occurs within the sanctuary precincts; LXX: ἐν τόπῳ ἁγίῳ (en topō hagiō, "in a holy place")] and he shall put on [ve-lavash] his garments [et-begadav] [et-begadav — "his garments"; the regular high-priestly golden vestments of Exod 28, resumed after the linen service; LXX: καὶ ἐνδύσεται τὴν στολὴν αὐτοῦ (kai endysetai tēn stolēn autou, "and shall put on his robe")] and he shall go out [ve-yatza'] [ve-yatza' — "and he shall go out"; LXX: καὶ ἐξελθὼν (kai exelthōn, "and going out")] and he shall make [ve-'asah] his burnt offering [et-olato] [et-olato — "his burnt offering / his olah"; the ram for the burnt offering of v.3; LXX: ποιήσει τὸ ὁλοκαύτωμα αὐτοῦ (poiēsei to holokautōma autou, "shall make his burnt offering")] and the burnt offering [ve-et-olat ha-am] of the people [ha-am] [ha-am — "the people"; the communal ram of v.5; LXX: καὶ τὸ ὁλοκαύτωμα τοῦ λαοῦ (kai to holokautōma tou laou, "and the burnt offering of the people")] — and he shall make atonement [ve-khipper] for himself [ba'ado] [ba'ado — as in vv.6, 11, 17; LXX: καὶ ἐξιλάσεται (kai exilasetai, "and shall make atonement")] and for the people [ve-ba'ad ha-am] [ve-ba'ad ha-am — "and for the people"; LXX: περὶ αὐτοῦ καὶ τοῦ λαοῦ (peri autou kai tou laou, "for himself and for the people")]:

**25** "And the fat [ve-et-chelev] of the sin offering [ha-chattat] [ve-et-chelev ha-chattat — "and the fat of the sin offering"; chelev = the specified suet/fatty portions (kidneys, liver lobe, etc.) of Lev 3 and 4; these are always burned on the altar; LXX: καὶ τὸ στέαρ τὸ περὶ τῆς ἁμαρτίας (kai to stear to peri tēs hamartias, "and the fat for sin"), using στέαρ (stear, "fat, suet, tallow")] he shall burn up [yaqtir] [yaqtir — "he shall burn up in smoke / shall turn to smoke"; hiphil of qatar: "to cause to smoke, to burn as an offering, to make sacrificial smoke"; the specified fat portions are burned on the altar; LXX: ἀνοίσει ἐπὶ τὸ θυσιαστήριον (anoisei epi to thysiastērion, "shall bring up upon the altar")] on the altar [ha-mizbeachah] [ha-mizbeachah — "on the altar"; the directional suffix; LXX: ἐπὶ τὸ θυσιαστήριον (epi to thysiastērion, "upon the altar")]:

**26** "And the one who sends away [ve-ha-meshallach] [ve-ha-meshallach — "and the one who sends away / and the sender"; piel participle of shalach: "the one who sends away, the dispatcher"; referring to the man designated in v.21 to lead the Azazel goat to the wilderness; LXX: καὶ ὁ ἐξαποστέλλων (kai ho exapostellōn, "and the one who sends away")] the goat for Azazel [et-ha-sa'ir la-Azazel] [et-ha-sa'ir la-Azazel — "the goat for Azazel"; as in vv.8–10; LXX: τὸν χίμαρον τὸν διεσταλμένον (ton chimaron ton diestatalmenon, "the goat that was separated/set apart") — the LXX does not reproduce Azazel here but uses a participle meaning "set apart, separated"] shall wash [yekabbes] his clothes [begadav] [yekabbes begadav — "shall launder his garments"; kibbes piel: as throughout ch.15; LXX: πλυνεῖ τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτοῦ (plynei ta himatia autou, "shall wash his garments")] and shall bathe [ve-rachatz] his flesh [et-besaro] in water [ba-mayim] [ve-rachatz et-besaro ba-mayim — "and shall bathe his flesh in water"; the standard laundering-bathing formula; LXX: καὶ λούσεται τὸ σῶμα αὐτοῦ ὕδατι (kai loussetai to sōma autou hydati, "and shall wash his body in water")] — and afterward [ve-achar-ken] [ve-achar-ken — "and after this / and afterward"; temporal marker; LXX: καὶ μετὰ ταῦτα (kai meta tauta, "and after these things")] he shall come [yavo] [yavo — "he shall come / shall enter"; LXX: εἰσελεύσεται (eiseleusetai, "shall enter")] into the camp [el-ha-machaneh] [el-ha-machaneh — "into the camp"; the community encampment; LXX: εἰς τὴν παρεμβολήν (eis tēn parembolēn, "into the encampment"), using παρεμβολή (parembolē, "encampment, camp")]:

**27** "And the bull of the sin offering [ve-et-par ha-chattat] [ve-et-par ha-chattat — "and the bull of the sin offering"; the bull of v.11 whose blood was brought inside; LXX: καὶ τὸν μόσχον τὸν περὶ τῆς ἁμαρτίας (kai ton moschon ton peri tēs hamartias, "and the calf for sin")] and the goat of the sin offering [ve-et-se'ir ha-chattat] [ve-et-se'ir ha-chattat — "and the goat of the sin offering"; the YHWH-lot goat of vv.9, 15; LXX: καὶ τὸν χίμαρον τὸν περὶ τῆς ἁμαρτίας (kai ton chimaron ton peri tēs hamartias, "and the young goat for sin")] whose blood was brought [asher huva dam'am] [asher huva dam'am — "whose blood was brought in"; huva = hophal of bo': "was brought in, was caused to enter"; dam'am = "their blood"; the bloods of both animals were brought into the Holy of Holies; LXX: ὧν εἰσηνέχθη τὸ αἷμα (hōn eisēnechthē to haima, "whose blood was brought in")] to make atonement [le-khapper] in the holy place [ba-qodesh] [ba-qodesh — "in the holy place"; inside the sanctuary; LXX: ἐξιλάσασθαι ἐν τῷ ἁγίῳ (exilasasthai en tō hagiō, "to make atonement in the holy")] shall be brought out [yotzi'] [yotzi' — "shall be brought out"; hiphil of yatza': "shall cause to go out, shall carry out"; the carcasses are carried outside; LXX: ἐξοίσουσιν αὐτά (exoisousin auta, "shall carry them out")] to outside the camp [el-mi-chuts la-machaneh] [el-mi-chuts la-machaneh — "to outside the camp / to the outside of the camp"; mi-chuts = "outside of, beyond the boundary of"; LXX: ἔξω τῆς παρεμβολῆς (exō tēs parembolēs, "outside the encampment")] and they shall burn [ve-sarfu] [ve-sarfu — "and they shall burn"; qal perfect of saraf: "to burn"; LXX: καὶ κατακαύσουσιν αὐτά (kai katakausousin auta, "and shall burn them completely")] with fire [ba-esh] their skins [et-orotam] [et-orotam — "their skins / their hides"; or (pl. orot) = "skin, hide"; the skins of both animals; LXX: ἐν πυρί, τὰ δέρματα αὐτῶν (en pyri, ta dermata autōn, "in fire, their hides")] and their flesh [ve-et-besaram] [ve-et-besaram — "and their flesh / their bodies"; LXX: καὶ τὰ κρέα αὐτῶν (kai ta krea autōn, "and their flesh")] and their dung [ve-et-pirsham] [ve-et-pirsham — "and their dung / their offal"; peresh = "dung, offal, refuse" — the intestinal contents; LXX: καὶ τὴν κόπρον αὐτῶν (kai tēn kopron autōn, "and their dung"), using κόπρος (kopros, "dung, refuse")]:

**28** "And the one who burns them [ve-ha-soref otam] [ve-ha-soref otam — "and the one burning them"; qal participle of saraf: "the burner, the one who burns"; as with the Azazel-man (v.26), the person who performs the burning incurs tum'ah; LXX: καὶ ὁ κατακαίων αὐτά (kai ho katakaiōn auta, "and the one burning them")] shall wash [yekabbes] his clothes [begadav] [yekabbes begadav — as in v.26; LXX: πλυνεῖ τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτοῦ (plynei ta himatia autou, "shall wash his garments")] and shall bathe [ve-rachatz] his flesh [et-besaro] in water [ba-mayim] — and afterward [ve-achar-ken] he shall come into the camp [yavo el-ha-machaneh] [yavo el-ha-machaneh — as in v.26; the formula is identical; LXX: καὶ εἰσελεύσεται εἰς τὴν παρεμβολήν (kai eiseleusetai eis tēn parembolēn, "and shall enter into the encampment")]:

---

**29** "And it shall be [ve-hayetah] [ve-hayetah — "and it shall be / and this shall be"; the transition to the permanent statute; LXX: καὶ ἔσται (kai estai, "and it shall be")] for you [la-khem] as a perpetual statute [le-chuqat olam] [le-chuqat olam — "as a statute of eternity / as a perpetual statute"; chuqat [חֻקַּת] = feminine construct of chuqqah ("statute, fixed ordinance"); olam = "eternity, perpetuity"; the phrase signals permanent, unrepeatable, binding legislation for all generations; LXX: νόμιμον αἰώνιον (nomimon aiōnion, "an eternal ordinance"), using νόμιμον (nomimon, "what is established by law, ordinance")] — in the seventh month [ba-chodesh ha-shevi'i] [ba-chodesh ha-shevi'i — "in the seventh month"; the seventh month of the Hebrew calendar is Tishri; the tenth of Tishri will become Yom Kippur; in the festival calendar of Lev 23:26–32, this date is also legislated with further detail; LXX: ἐν τῷ μηνὶ τῷ ἑβδόμῳ (en tō mēni tō hebdomō, "in the seventh month")] on the tenth of the month [be-'asor la-chodesh] [be-'asor la-chodesh — "on the tenth of the month"; asor = "ten, tenth"; LXX: τῇ δεκάτῃ τοῦ μηνός (tē dekatē tou mēnos, "on the tenth of the month")] you shall afflict your souls [te'annu et-nafshoteikhem] [te'annu et-nafshoteikhem — "you shall afflict your souls / you shall humble your beings"; innah piel of 'anah: "to afflict, to humble, to oppress, to cause suffering to"; nafshoteikhem = "your souls / your beings / your selves" (nefesh as the whole person, the living being); the phrase ve-innitem et-nafshoteikhem = the prescribed affliction of Yom Kippur, traditionally interpreted as fasting (and associated abstentions); the precise definition of "affliction of the soul" is debated in later tradition but fasting is the primary understanding; LXX: ταπεινώσετε τὰς ψυχὰς ὑμῶν (tapeinōsete tas psychas hymōn, "you shall humble your souls"), using ταπεινόω (tapeinoō, "to humble, to abase, to afflict")] and all [ve-khol-] work [melakhah] you shall not do [lo' ta'asu] [ve-khol-melakhah lo' ta'asu — "and all work you shall not do / and no work shall you perform"; melakhah = "labor, work, craft"; the prohibition of labor on Yom Kippur; LXX: καὶ πᾶν ἔργον οὐ ποιήσετε (kai pan ergon ou poiēsete, "and every work you shall not do")] — the native [ha-ezrach] [ha-ezrach — "the native / the native-born"; ezrach = the citizen-born Israelite, as opposed to the ger (sojourner); LXX: ὁ αὐτόχθων (ho autochtōn, "the native-born"), using αὐτόχθων (autochtōn, "born from the very earth, indigenous, native")] and the sojourner [ve-ha-ger] [ve-ha-ger — "and the sojourner / the resident alien"; ger [גֵּר] = the resident foreigner, the one who has come to dwell among Israel; the inclusion of the ger in Yom Kippur obligations is significant; LXX: καὶ ὁ προσήλυτος (kai ho prosēlytos, "and the proselyte/sojourner"), using προσήλυτος (prosēlytos, "one who has come over, a proselyte")] who sojourns [ha-gar] in your midst [be-tokheikhem] [ha-gar be-tokheikhem — "who sojourns in your midst / who lives among you"; qal participle of gur: "the one dwelling as ger"; LXX: ὁ προσκείμενος ἐν ὑμῖν (ho proskeimenoi en hymin, "the one attached among you")]:

**30** "For on this day [ki va-yom ha-zeh] [ki va-yom ha-zeh — "for on this day"; the theological grounding of the statute; LXX: ἐν γὰρ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ ταύτῃ (en gar tē hēmera tautē, "for on this day")] atonement shall be made for you [yekhapper aleikhem] [yekhapper aleikhem — "he shall make atonement for you / atonement shall be made for you"; pual or qal with YHWH as implicit subject: "it shall atone for you"; the verse is the theological climax of the entire chapter; LXX: ἐξιλάσεται περὶ ὑμῶν (exilasetai peri hymōn, "shall make atonement for you")] to cleanse you [le-taher ethem] [le-taher ethem — "to cleanse you / to purify you"; piel infinitive of taher: "to make clean, to purify, to pronounce clean"; LXX: καθαρίσαι ὑμᾶς (katharisai hymas, "to purify you")] — from all your sins [mi-kol-chatto'teikhem] [mi-kol-chatto'teikhem — "from all your sins"; LXX: ἀπὸ πασῶν τῶν ἁμαρτιῶν ὑμῶν (apo pasōn tōn hamartiōn hymōn, "from all your sins")] before the LORD [lifnei YHWH] [lifnei YHWH — "before the LORD / in the presence of the LORD"; the atonement is accomplished before YHWH — it is not merely a human ritual but a divinely effected act; LXX: ἔναντι κυρίου (enanti kyriou, "before the LORD")] you shall be clean [tit'haru] [tit'haru — "you shall be clean"; hithpa'el or qal imperfect: "shall become clean, shall be cleansed"; the result of the day's rites; LXX: καθαρισθήσεσθε (katharisthēsesthe, "you shall be cleansed")]:

**31** "It is a sabbath of solemn rest [shabbat shabbaton hi'] [shabbat shabbaton hi' — "a sabbath of solemn-rest, it is"; the doubled root: shabbat + shabbaton; shabbaton [שַׁבָּתוֹן] is the intensive/abstract noun from shabbat ("to cease, to rest"): "a resting, a complete cessation, a total rest"; the doubled formula shabbat shabbaton is the most emphatic statement of sabbath-rest in the Torah — it occurs only here (16:31) and in the legislation of Lev 23:32 (also for Yom Kippur) and in the Jubilee (25:4); for ordinary weekly Sabbaths the Torah uses shabbaton or shabbat alone, never the doubled formula; the doubling elevates the Day of Atonement above regular Sabbaths as a "Sabbath of sabbaths / a super-sabbath / a sabbath of solemn rest"; LXX: σάββατα σαββάτων (sabbata sabbatōn, "a sabbath of sabbaths"), using the Greek genitive plural to render the superlative; English translations: "a sabbath of complete rest" (NJPS), "a sabbath of solemn rest" (ESV/RSV), "a sabbath of sabbaths" (Young's Literal)] — for you [la-khem] [la-khem — "for you / belonging to you"; the day is given to Israel; LXX: ὑμῖν (hymin, "for you")] and you shall afflict your souls [ve-innitem et-nafshoteikhem] [ve-innitem et-nafshoteikhem — as in v.29; the same formula repeated in the permanent statute; LXX: καὶ ταπεινώσετε τὰς ψυχὰς ὑμῶν (kai tapeinōsete tas psychas hymōn, "and you shall humble your souls")] as a perpetual statute [chuqat olam] [chuqat olam — as in v.29; the permanent, eternal character of the statute; LXX: νόμιμον αἰώνιον (nomimon aiōnion, "an eternal ordinance")]:

**32** "And the priest [ve-khipper ha-kohen] [ve-khipper ha-kohen — "and the priest who makes atonement"; literally "and the priest shall make atonement"; LXX: καὶ ἐξιλάσεται ὁ ἱερεύς (kai exilasetai ho hiereus, "and the priest shall make atonement")] who is anointed [asher-yimshach oto] [asher-yimshach oto — "who shall be anointed / who has been anointed"; qal or pual of mashach: "to anoint"; the anointing of the High Priest is in view; LXX: ὃν ἂν χρίσωσιν αὐτόν (hon an chrisōsin auton, "whom they shall anoint")] and who is consecrated [va-asher yemalle et-yado] [va-asher yemalle et-yado — "and who shall fill his hand / who is consecrated"; the idiom male' yad ("to fill the hand") = "to consecrate, to install, to ordain" (as in Exod 28:41, 29:9; Lev 8:33); the filling of the hand with the offerings of installation; LXX: καὶ οὗ ἐπληρώθη ἡ χεὶρ αὐτοῦ (kai hou eplērōthē hē cheir autou, "and whose hand was filled"), using πληρόω (plēroō, "to fill")] to minister as priest [le-khahen] [le-khahen — "to serve as priest / to function as priest"; piel infinitive of kahan: "to act as priest"; LXX: ἱερατεύειν (hierateuein, "to serve as priest, to exercise the priestly office")] in the place of his father [tachat aviv] [tachat aviv — "in the place of his father / in succession to his father"; tachat = "beneath, in place of, succeeding"; the succession: the next high priest who follows his father in office; LXX: ἀντὶ τοῦ πατρὸς αὐτοῦ (anti tou patros autou, "in place of his father")] — he shall make atonement [ve-khipper] [ve-khipper — the verb governing the rest of the verse; LXX: ποιήσει τὴν ἐξίλασιν ταύτην (poiēsei tēn exilasin tautēn, "shall make this atonement")] and shall put on [ve-lavesh] the linen garments [et-bigdei ha-bad] [et-bigdei ha-bad — as in v.23; LXX: ἐνδύσεται τὴν στολὴν τὴν λινῆν (endysetai tēn stolēn tēn linēn, "shall put on the linen vestment")] the holy garments [bigdei ha-qodesh] [bigdei ha-qodesh — "the holy garments"; **33** "And he shall make atonement for [ve-khipper et] the holy sanctuary [miqdashe ha-qodesh] [miqdashe ha-qodesh — "the sanctuary of holiness / the most holy sanctuary"; miqdashe = construct plural or intensive of miqdash ("sanctuary, holy place, sanctuary"): "the holy place's holiness"; some render it "the inner sanctuary" or "the most holy place"; LXX: τοῦ ἁγίου ναοῦ (tou hagiou naou, "the holy temple/sanctuary"), using ναός (naos, "shrine, temple, inner sanctuary")] and for the tent of meeting [ve-et-ohel mo'ed] and for the altar [ve-et-ha-mizbeath] [ve-et-ha-mizbeath — the same three loci as vv.16–19: Holy of Holies, Tent of Meeting, Altar; LXX: καὶ τῆς σκηνῆς τοῦ μαρτυρίου καὶ τοῦ θυσιαστηρίου (kai tēs skēnēs tou martyriou kai tou thysiastēriou, "and the tent of testimony and the altar")] he shall make atonement for — and for the priests [ve-al-ha-kohanim] [ve-al-ha-kohanim — "and for the priests"; the priestly class as a whole; LXX: καὶ περὶ τῶν ἱερέων (kai peri tōn hiereōn, "and for the priests")] and for all the people [ve-al-kol-am] of the congregation [ha-qahal] [ve-al-kol-am ha-qahal — "and for all the people of the assembly / and for the entire congregation"; qahal as in v.17; LXX: καὶ περὶ παντὸς πλήθους τῆς συναγωγῆς (kai peri pantos plēthous tēs synagōgēs, "and for the whole multitude of the congregation"), using πλῆθος (plēthos, "multitude, full number")] he shall make atonement:

## EMET's Textual Notes

### Manuscript Observations

**Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 67v–68r:** The text of Leviticus 16 occupies the bottom of folio 67v and the recto of folio 68r. The chapter is one of the most carefully presented in the Leningrad Codex: the Tiberian vocalization and cantillation marks are meticulously applied throughout, enabling precise grammatical analysis of the chapter's extensive ritual vocabulary. The chapter opens without a new parasha marker immediately following ch.15's close (the narrative transition of v.1 — "after the death of the sons of Aharon" — provides the contextual bridge). Major parasha divisions appear before v.29 (the permanent statute section) and before v.34 (the colophon). The vocalization of Azazel [עֲזָאזֵל] — with patach under the alef, tsere under the zayin, patach under the second zayin, and tsere under the lamed — is consistent and unambiguous in the LC, representing the Tiberian tradition's reading without variant. The technical term kapporet [כַּפֹּרֶת] is also consistently vocalized. The phrase shabbat shabbaton (v.31) is written with clear distinctive cantillation marks emphasizing both words. The three-part sin vocabulary in vv.16 and 21 (tum'ot, pish'im, chatto'ot / avonot, pish'im, chatto'ot) is marked by distinctive phrasing and cantillation in the manuscript.

**Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), Pages 102–103:** The LXX of ch.16 across these two Vaticanus pages presents a generally close rendering of the MT, with several notable translation choices. The most significant is the treatment of Azazel: the LXX consistently uses ἀποπομπαῖος (apompaios, "the Averter, the Sender-away, the one who removes evil") in v.8, and ἀποπομπή (apompē, "the sending away") in v.10, and omits the Azazel-reference in v.26 entirely, replacing it with a participle. This suggests the LXX translator understood Azazel as a concept of "sending away / removal" rather than a proper name or demon. The LXX also adds directional detail in v.14 (specifying eastward sprinkling on the kapporet), renders shabbat shabbaton as σάββατα σαββάτων ("a sabbath of sabbaths"), and uses ἱλαστήριον (hilastērion) consistently for kapporet — the word Paul will deploy in Romans 3:25 of the death of Jesus.

---

### Key Translation Decisions

1. **kapporet [כַּפֹּרֶת]** (vv.2, 13, 14, 15): Preserved untranslated. The kapporet is the golden lid of the ark of the testimony (Exod 25:17–22), flanked by the two golden cherubim, above which YHWH appears and speaks. It is both the physical cover of the ark and the theological "atonement-place." English traditions render it "mercy seat" (Tyndale, Luther: Gnadenstuhl) or "atonement cover" (NIV). Neither fully captures the term: "mercy seat" is interpretive and post-Reformational; "atonement cover" is explanatory rather than translational. EMET preserves the Hebrew term. The LXX ἱλαστήριον (hilastērion) passes into the NT at Romans 3:25 and Hebrews 9:5.

2. **Azazel [עֲזָאזֵל]** (vv.8, 10, 26): Preserved absolutely untranslated. This is one of the most contested terms in the entire Hebrew Bible. Four main interpretations exist: (a) A wilderness demon or spirit (ancient Near Eastern background; 1 Enoch identifies Azazel as a fallen angel of Watchers; Origen, Tertullian, and some modern scholars support this reading); (b) A place name meaning "rough ground" or "steep mountain" (the goat is sent to a rocky place); (c) An abstract noun meaning "complete removal / complete sending away" (from 'ez ["goat"] + 'azal ["to go away"]: "the going-away goat"); (d) An intensive form of 'azal ("to remove entirely"): "the complete removal." The LXX chose option (c)/(d) with ἀποπομπαῖος ("the Averter/Sender-away"). The Mishnah (Yoma 6) describes a specific cliff-site called Bet Hadudo where the goat was pushed off; the Talmud and later tradition developed elaborate Azazel theology. The parallelism la-YHWH / la-Azazel in v.8 is grammatically striking: whatever Azazel is, it stands in grammatical parallel with YHWH as the recipient of a lot-designated goat. This does not imply Azazel is equal to YHWH, but it does suggest Azazel is a genuine entity or destination that "receives" the sin-laden goat. EMET preserves Azazel as a proper term requiring no interpretation by the translator.

3. **goral [גּוֹרָל]** (vv.8, 9, 10): "lot." The physical drawing of lots — casting marked stones or sticks — to determine the divine will. Rendered "lot" without elaboration.

4. **ve-hitvaddah [וְהִתְוַדָּה]** (v.21): "he shall confess." The hithpa'el of yadah: the reflexive-intensive of the "acknowledgment/praise" root. In the hithpa'el it means "to confess, to acknowledge one's sins upon oneself." The act involves Aharon pressing both hands (not one hand, as in ordinary sacrificial laying-on) on the goat's head, transferring by speech-act all Israel's iniquities, transgressions, and sins.

5. **shabbat shabbaton [שַׁבַּת שַׁבָּתוֹן]** (v.31): "a sabbath of solemn rest." The doubled root-form is unique as applied to Yom Kippur (it appears also at 23:32 for the same day, and at 25:4 for the land's sabbath in the Jubilee year). EMET renders it "sabbath of solemn rest" following the RSV/ESV tradition while noting the LXX's superlative rendering σάββατα σαββάτων ("sabbath of sabbaths").

6. **ve-innitem et-nafshoteikhem [וְעִנִּיתֶם אֶת-נַפְשֹׁתֵיכֶם]** (vv.29, 31): "you shall afflict your souls." Innah piel = "to afflict, to humble, to cause suffering to." The object is nafshoteikhem, "your souls/beings/selves" (nefesh as the holistic person). The rabbinic tradition specified the affliction as five abstentions: fasting (no eating or drinking), no bathing, no anointing with oil, no wearing leather shoes, and no conjugal relations. The text itself does not enumerate these; "afflict your souls" is the Hebrew's intentionally encompassing phrase.

7. **The three sin-terms** (vv.16, 21): The triad tum'ot / pish'im / chatto'ot (v.16) and avonot / pish'im / chatto'ot (v.21) represents a comprehensive taxonomy of human moral and ritual failure. Tum'ot = ritual impurities (the subject of chs.11–15). Pesha'/pish'im = transgressions, rebellions, deliberate defections (the most willful category). Chatto'ot = sins (the broadest category, from chata' "to miss the mark"). Avonot (v.21) = iniquities, twistedness, guilt (the deeply embedded moral failure). Together these three in v.21 cover the totality of human sinfulness — inadvertent, deliberate, and deeply-rooted.

8. **itti [עִתִּי]** (v.21): "the appointed/prepared man." The hapax or near-hapax itti = "one of/for the time/occasion," a person specifically designated for the task of leading the Azazel goat to the wilderness. Later tradition (Mishnah Yoma) elaborated this role extensively: the man was attended by leading citizens from Jerusalem to the edge of the wilderness, where he released the goat on a ledge.

9. **eretz gezarah [אֶרֶץ גְּזֵרָה]** (v.22): "a land cut off." Gezarah from gazar ("to cut, to separate"): "a land of cutting/separation, an inaccessible region." The LXX renders it as ἄβατος (abatos, "impassable, trackless") — a desolate place beyond human habitation. The term suggests not merely desert but a region beyond the bounds of the habitable world, a place of total exile.

10. **miqdashe ha-qodesh [מִקְדַּשׁ הַקֹּדֶשׁ]** (v.33): The construct phrase "sanctuary of holiness" is a superlative (the holiest sanctuary / the most holy sanctuary), distinguished from ha-qodesh (the holy place) used throughout the chapter. It functions here as the summary term for the entire sanctuary complex being served by the atonement rites.
